name:
Action for Resilience, Climate and Health Collaborative at USCRecognized Student Organization (RSO)
							 
								- Civic Engagement, Health & Wellness, Service, Environmental/SustainabilityWebsiteMissionMembership BenefitsContact:David Rios PalomaresMissionARCH SC’s mission aligns with that of nonprofit ARCH Collaborative: to advance innovative, evidence‑based solutions that build community resilience against chronic disease and extreme weather. We believe that addressing chronic disease and climate‑driven disasters requires integrating public health, environmental justice and community empowerment. Our chapter will bring people together to design and disseminate practical tools and educational programs. Some examples are teaching residents how to engage in policymaking and environmental justice, distributing supplies and resources to help them adapt to extreme weather, and mentoring the next generation of leaders through the ARCH Scholars Program. We are committed to grounding our work in rigorous research by examining links between environmental exposures and health outcomes and assessing the impact of our programs. Ultimately, our mission is to create healthier, more resilient communities by fostering collaboration, innovation and inclusion.Membership BenefitsBecoming a member of ARCH SC offers rich professional development and service opportunities. 501(c)3 non-profit, ARCH Collaborative, prides itself on fostering an inclusive culture and investing in our members’ development as future leaders in climate and health. As part of the USC student chapter of ARCH Collaborative, members will gain hands‑on experience through community engagement programs, such as workshops that teach Los Angeles residents how to advance health and environmental justice, and the Community Climate Adaptation Program, which supplies resources to help residents (particularly unhoused individuals) prepare for extreme weather. Members can also participate in the ARCH Scholars Program, where they serve as mentors and train LA residents to design evidence‑based interventions for health and the environment. Research opportunities include analyzing links between environmental exposures and health outcomes and evaluating program impacts, allowing members to contribute to cutting‑edge knowledge while building practical skills. Overall, membership offers networking with dedicated professionals, leadership experience, and the chance to make a tangible difference in community resilience.
mission: ARCH SC’s mission aligns with that of nonprofit ARCH Collaborative: to advance innovative, evidence‑based solutions that build community resilience against chronic disease and extreme weather. We believe that addressing chronic disease and climate‑driven disasters requires integrating public health,

ACDT students via attending LA composites conferences (e.g. SAMPE, and CAMX), will build their professional networks via LA local aerospace composites industries e.g. Boeing, Lockheed Martin, Aerospace Corp, and Northrop Grumman, for their career development. name:
Advanced Degree Consulting Club at USCRecognized Student Organization (RSO)
							 
								- Pre-Professional, AcademicWebsiteMissionMembership BenefitsContact:Riley Elmer,Email group officersMissionAdvanced Degree Consulting Club (ADCC) serves advanced degree candidates (with a specific focus on PhD and Master students) that are passionate about pursuing management consulting as their career path.Membership BenefitsAs a member of the Advanced Degree Consulting Club, you will be stepping into a vibrant community designed to accelerate your professional growth and development. This isn’t just a club—it’s a powerful network where your advanced academic background will be transformed into practical expertise. Here, you’ll sharpen your analytical and problem-solving skills through rigorous training sessions and hands-on case studies, all while leveraging the deep knowledge and experience of fellow members who share your advanced academic journey.But the benefits go far beyond skill-building. You’ll gain exclusive access to a network of industry leaders from both the for-profit and nonprofit sectors—professionals who truly understand the challenges and opportunities of transitioning from academia to consulting. Through real-world consulting projects, you’ll apply your theoretical insights in ways that make a tangible impact, while frequent, constructive feedback from peers and mentors ensures your continuous personal and professional development.Additionally, the club’s robust resources—including industry reports, interview preparation sessions, and more—are strategically designed to help you navigate and excel in the consulting world. Joining the Advanced Degree Consulting Club isn’t just about enhancing your resume; it’s about embarking on a transformative journey that bridges the academic world with the fast-paced, dynamic field of consulting, setting you up for long-term success.
mission: Advanced Degree Consulting Club (ADCC) serves advanced degree candidates (with a specific focus on PhD and Master students) that are passionate about pursuing management consulting as their career path.

name:
Art.IficialRecognized Student Organization (RSO)
							 
								- Academic, Media/Publications, CareerWebsiteMissionMembership BenefitsMissionThe purpose of the club is to bring together students interested in the intersection of AI, entertainment, art, media, design, technology, and business. Members of this club will learn about AI’s impact on creative fields, engage with prominent guest speakers, discuss the ethics and concerns of these technological advancements, learn technical skills involving creative AI tools, and implement what they learn from the club in final capstone projects. This club will be the first at USC to explore the nexus of where creativity meets artificial intelligence and how technological advancements will impact the future of the creator economy.As an example, some potential topics this club could explore are, but not limited to, "The impact of video generation tools on filmmaking", "Is the AI generated Drake song legal and/or ethical?", "How to use ChatGPT to get over writer's block."Membership BenefitsThere are 4 key benefits to being an Art.Ificial member:1. Learn about various topics related to the intersection of AI and M&E, the arts, and other creative fields through the guest speaker series.2. Build connections with people also interested in media, business, and tech through our social gatherings.3. Develop technical skills in various AI software and tools to help enhance creative endeavors.4. Create a capstone project that can demonstrate what you have learned from the club.

								- Ethnic/Cultural, Pre-Professional, Recreation, Spiritual, Career, SocialWebsiteMissionMembership BenefitsContact:Veer Vora,Arnav Rupesh Banthia,Email group officersMissionYou bring with you everything that makes India so uniquely Indian: the rich culture, the poetic art, the aromatic food, and the engaging people. You bring with you the power of meditation, the Yoga and the Ayurveda, the Taj Mahal and the Gateway of Indiaa, Bollywood films andthe Bachchans, the Ambassadors and AutoRickshaws, the IPL and the Tendulkar, the Akbar and the Birbal, the Sitar and the Tabla. You bring with you Raaga, Nritya, Natya, the politics, the weddings, one thousand dialects, 300 different ways of cooking the potato, and yet you bring with you the power of a single identity.We at Association of Indian Students indulge in everything Indian you bring, and locate amidst our diversities what we all have in common. We dance away to desi beats, splash colors on our faces, gulp those gol gappas off a saucer, sing the national anthem, and watch with bated breath as Dhoni smashes that last ball into the stands. And until the summons from home becomes too loud to ignore, we keep you connected to your roots.Come, let us recapture that thrill that runs through each of us when we think of home. Welcome to the Association of Indian Students at the University of Southern California.Membership BenefitsMembers can attend the meetings and participate in the events

name: Birnkrant Residential CollegeResidential EducationWebsiteMissionContact:Derek Murakami (he/him/his)MissionConstructed in 1963, Birnkrant has been a part of USC over the last several decades. Created in honor of Cecele & Michael G. Birnkrant, the eight-story tower originally provided housing for 308 women. Over the many years of Birnkrant’s existence on the University Park campus, co-ed floors were created, and in 2011, the community began functioning as a first-year Honors community. First-year Presidential, Trustee, & Mork Scholars live in Birnkrant’s tight-knit community after an application and interview process. Commonly referred to as “8 Floors of Open Doors,” the Birnkrant community is strong, as friendships and relationships are created that often span the course of the students’ time at USC. In 1994, Birnkrant began operating as a Residential College, with a full-time, live-in Faculty Master residing in the building and working to bridge the experiences inside and outside the classroom. The Faculty Master works alongside Residential Education staff to provide programs that introduce students to Los Angeles culture, art, food, and more. Traditionally, Birnkrant residents move in two days prior to the campus-wide move-in day, to attend a retreat with the community, faculty, and staff. This retreat often includes community building activities and an off-campus trip to the Hollywood Bowl for a performance by the LA Philharmonic. Residents take great pride in living in Birnkrant and often refer to it as one of their favorite memories and experiences as a USC student.Membership Benefits

name:
Brothers Breaking B.R.E.A.D.Recognized Student Organization (RSO)
							 
								- Ethnic/Cultural, Pre-Professional, Health & Wellness, Recreation, Club Sport, Visual & Performing Arts, Career, Service, SocialWebsiteMissionMembership BenefitsContact:Email group officersMissionBrothers Breaking B.R.E.A.D. (BBB) is a pioneering student organization founded by black men at the University of Southern California, united by the principles of Brotherhood, Empowerment, Health, Leadership, Service, and Enrichment. Our mission, encapsulated in the acronym 'B.R.E.A.D.' — Barriers, Regrets, Egos, Animosity, and Doubt, is to break down these obstacles and cultivate a space where black men on campus can come together in brotherly fellowship.BBB serves as the nucleus of black male excellence, striving to empower and inspire our members to embrace leadership roles while redefining masculinity to address critical issues within the black community, including hyper-masculinity, misogynoir, and homophobia. Our organization warmly welcomes anyone who identifies as a black man, transcending all intersections within this identity.Our diverse membership enriches our learning experience, enabling individuals to advocate for themselves and each other. BBB is dedicated to fostering professional and leadership skills, while dismantling stereotypes and stigmas. Our ultimate aim is to become a permanent and transformative presence at the University of Southern California, leaving an enduring legacy of unity, empowerment, and positive change.Membership BenefitsBecoming a member of Brothers Breaking B.R.E.A.D. (BBB) offers the chance to grow personally and professionally within a strong community of driven Black men. Members gain access to mentorship, networking, and professional development opportunities through partnerships with other organizations and companies BBB has connections to. The organization also provides leadership pathways whether through executive board positions, event planning, or community initiatives that help members build confidence and real-world skills. Beyond career growth, BBB fosters brotherhood, emotional support, and a safe space for open dialogue, creating a foundation for lifelong connections and collective empowerment.
categories: Brothers Breaking B.R.E.A.D. (BBB) is a pioneering student organization founded by black men at the University of Southern California
united by the principles of Brotherhood
is to break down these obstacles and cultivate a space where black men on campus can come together in brotherly fellowship.

name: Cale & Irani Residential CollegesResidential EducationWebsiteMissionContact:Dionely AcostaMissionCale Residential College for Global Perspectives, engages students in the complexities of the world by immersing yourself in global exploration at USC. In this Residential College, you will build your intercultural competency and participate in critical dialogue regarding the ever changing global landscape. Take part in partnerships with academic departments and resource centers that focus on international affairs, business, arts/culture, media, and current events. Discuss critical issues regarding the world’s refugee crisis, hunger, poverty, socio-political strife, and global citizenry. Students will emerge from this Residential College Experience as global citizens ready to make a positive impact on the world. Irani Residential College for Performance Science focuses on the mind, body, and business. Students in this residential college will get to explore their untapped potential in learning how to break down barriers inhibiting leadership, decision-making, resilience, or creative potential. Students will attend programs and workshops that will help them understand brain and body function, human behavior, psychology, emotion, and leadership, all with the goals of maximizing performance, both individually and within an organization or community.Membership Benefits

name: Cardinal GardensResidential EducationWebsiteMissionContact:Shamin;Mason Harrington;MasonMissionCardinal Gardens is a popular apartment option among continuing undergraduate students. The community consists of nine individual townhouse-style buildings connected by winding, tree-lined walkways. Residents can choose between fully furnished one or two-bedroom apartments, each with a full kitchen, bathroom and a spacious, air-conditioned living room. Located on the north side of Jefferson Boulevard, Cardinal Gardens is across the street from campus and near to both the Lyon Recreation Center and Café 84. The newly renovated central courtyard provides a great gathering place for residents, where they can relax around the modern fountain in comfortable couches and chairs, grill on two gas barbecues, or spend a quiet evening sitting next to the large outdoor fireplace. Residents can also enjoy playing half-court basketball or lounging in the Customer Service Center, equipped with a 70 inch flat screen TV and pool and foosball tables.Membership Benefits

name:

								- Academic, Health & Wellness, Media/PublicationsWebsiteMissionMembership BenefitsContact:Mugil Shanmugam,Sophia HameedMissionOur mission is to improve medical literacy in both the USC and greater LA metropolitan area. We aim to do this through a bi-annual magazine that explores pressing medical issues and the resources available to quail such problems.The magazine will be an undergraduate magazine that addresses medically relevant issues, education, research, and art. The magazine will comprise of four sections:1) novel medical research within the USC community (in the form of interviews, overviews of current USC research and published articles/journals),2) explaining pertinent technology within the emerging medical field (this could be something as simple as "What is CRISPR" to explaining "How models of empathy are built and monitored"),3) any artistic mediums that are inspired by medicine and its practice (poems, personal-essays, dance, etc.),4) and lastly, the education portion, which would aim at elucidating any college-specific health related issues (mental health resources on campus, STD treatment on campus, updates on COVID-19 conditions on campus and the respective measures in place). Furthermore, the magazine will be translated into Spanish, so that we are able to reach LA's Spanish-speaking population.Membership BenefitsMembership is cohort-based, so one is able to benefit from a close knit community, amazing writing opportunities, and improving the multiple communities (e.g. South LA, USC, and Spanish speakers) through medical education. Likewise, there is always the prospect of cool merchandise.

name:
Emergency Medicine Student Interest GroupRecognized Student Organization (RSO)
							 
								- Pre-Professional, Academic, CareerWebsiteMissionContact:Email group officersMissionThe Emergency Medicine Student Interest Group (EMSIG), organized in 1995, has grown to become the largest student interest group at the Keck School of Medicine. EMSIG operates within the Department of Emergency Medicine at Los Angeles General Hospital under the direct guidance of Dr. Taku Taira, the organization’s advisor and faculty mentor. Through partnership with the Department of Emergency Medicine, EMSIG offers medical students hands-on clinical experience, mentorship with residents and faculty members, procedure-based workshops, and research opportunities.EMSIG attracts a large number of first- and second-year medical students because its shadowing program allows students to gain early clinical experience in one of the country’s busiest and most prestigious emergency departments. Owing to the success of this shadowing program is the enthusiasm that the attending physicians and residents in L.A. General’s emergency department have for teaching. Faculty members from the Emergency Department also give monthly lunchtime lectures to introduce medical students to topics of interest ranging from head trauma to shark bites and lead workshops in techniques such as suturing and intubation.The Department of Emergency Medicine has been generous in offering mentorship through EMSIG in a variety of ways. Students may be paired up with a faculty mentor early in their medical school careers. EMSIG’s annual end-of-the-year reception also gives medical students the chance to talk with fourth-year-students who just completed the match process, residents, and faculty members from the Department of Emergency Medicine. Mentors are just as willing to help students navigate their course toward finding a specialty of interest as they are in sharing their own interest in Emergency Medicine. EMSIG also offers medical students the chance to attend several Emergency Medicine conferences.Regardless of which specialty medical students in EMSIG end up choosing, members of EMSIG credit the interest group in playing an important role in providing clinical experience in an exciting field and in helping them make personal career decisions.Membership Benefits
mission: The Emergency Medicine Student Interest Group (EMSIG), organized in 1995, has grown to become the largest student interest group at the Keck School of Medicine. EMSIG operates within the Department of Emergency Medicine at Los Angeles General Hospital under the direct guidance of Dr. Taku Taira, the

								- Health & Wellness, RecreationWebsiteMissionMembership BenefitsMissionOur mission is to cultivate a vibrant community that explores and celebrates the world of fermentation together. Together, we'll uncover the mysteries and benefits of this ancient culinary tradition.Membership BenefitsJoining our Fermentation Interest Group provides a hands-on experience with the art and science of fermentation. Beyond the jars and microbes, the group fosters a community of like-minded enthusiasts, sharing in tasting sessions, troubleshooting, and the joy of homemade, probiotic-rich creations.

We want to quite literally 'break bread' with our peers and engage with our student body through a shared enthusiasm and curiosity of our respective cuisine and cultures. Trying different or new foods can be a whole adventure, it can be daunting yet very exciting. We want to encourage people to open their minds to trying new things because after all you never know what you'll really like until you try it.Our main initiative is to spark conversation, in a world that nowadays is so divided and defined by labels, we wanted to create an open atmosphere where people can transcend cultural barriers and truly get to know one another. We want to break the ice to initiate a more engaging dialogue where people from all walks of life can come together to share and discuss their cultures, values, passions and just have a good conversation.Membership Benefits

Our mission is to empower aspiring physicians with the tools, guidance, and community necessary to reach the medical school of their dreams and ultimately serve others through a career in medicine. Through mentorship, educational programming, and accessible opportunities, we aim to create an inclusive and uplifting environment where all students can thrive on their path to becoming compassionate, capable healthcare providers.Membership BenefitsMembership in our RSO provides pre-medical students with meaningful resources and opportunities to support their journey toward medical school. Benefits include MCAT preparation through group study sessions and shared materials, as well as virtual Q&A sessions with admissions officers from top medical schools like UCLA and Columbia. Members also gain access to peer mentorship, pre-med planning support, and guidance on clinical exposure, application timelines, and personal statements. The organization fosters a collaborative environment where students can connect, learn, and grow together at every stage of their pre-med path.

As an organization, we strive to provide students with hands-on volunteering and shadowing opportunities to bring attention to public health disparities and improve worldwide health care sustainability. During break, our organization travels to rural communities of either Guatemala, Honduras, Belize, Greece, Panama, and Ghana to volunteer among medical professionals to provide essential medical services. During the semester, we hold medical workshops, fundraisers, general body meetings, volunteering events, guest speakers, and socials to build our GMB community on campus.Membership BenefitsMembers have the opportunity to partake in an international movement to implement sustainable healthcare systems and increase access to quality healthcare. Members will be trained in healthcare related topics, cultural competency, and global health issues to equip them to make a difference in any healthcare or clinical setting they may find themselves in. Members will have access to networking opportunities with similarly passionate health equity advocates, pre-health students, pre-professional students and healthcare professionals. They will also have access to pre-health skills workshops, keynote presentations with healthcare professionals and grad student guest speakers. Members will have access to volunteer opportunities that utilize the DEI, pre-health, and cultural competency training they receive to impact our local community. Furthermore, members will have the opportunity to apply to partake in our annual medical brigades to Global Brigades free health clinics in partner countries, such as Guatemala, Honduras, Belize, Greece, Panama and Ghana.

name: Graduate and Family HousingResidential EducationWebsiteMissionContact:Hector Solis-OrtizMissionGraduate and Family Housing is endearingly referred to as GradFam. USC offers approximately 2,000 spaces for graduate students and students with families. Since the housing needs of graduate students and families typically differ from those of undergraduate students, Graduate and Family Housing is located in the smaller buildings in the North University Park area. Graduate student housing offers a variety of options from single occupancy studios and apartments that allow students to live and study on their own to the lower cost option of shared bedrooms and apartments units. Family Housing mainly consists of one-bedroom apartments, but there are a few two-bedroom apartments for those families that require a little more space. There are several amenities within the graduate and family communities including lounge spaces, gyms, courtyards, and play areas. We offer live in staff including Resident Assistants, a Residential Community Coordinator, and a Faculty in Residence who put on events, provide nightly emergency assistance, and support resident development.Membership Benefits

Our mission is to promote academic excellence, professional growth, and community engagement by serving as the collective voice of our student body to faculty and program administration. Through organized academic, networking, and career development initiatives, HBRSA fosters opportunities for intellectual and professional advancement. Additionally, we aim to build a vibrant and supportive community by facilitating social and volunteer activities, enriching student life and creating lasting connections among peers. HBRSA is committed to enhancing the graduate experience, advancing the Health Behavior Research field, and nurturing the success of all HBR students at USC.Membership BenefitsAs a member of HBRSA, you gain access to a wealth of academic, professional, and networking resources tailored to support your growth in Health Behavior Research Ph.D. program. Members enjoy exclusive career development events, skill-building workshops, and opportunities to connect with faculty and industry professionals. Beyond academic and professional advantages, HBRSA fosters a supportive community through social events and volunteer activities that help build lasting friendships and connections. By joining, you’ll be part of a network that actively enhances the graduate student experience and advocates for the needs and success of all Health Behavior Research students at USC. All USC students, staff, faculty, and alumni are eligible to be members. Open membership allows individuals outside of the Health Behavior Research Ph.D. program to engage with our community and contribute to our mission of fostering academic, professional, and social development.

Training: The USC Human Rights Student Interest Group hosts nationwide training sessions for physicians to gain the skills necessary to perform forensic evaluations and register under PHR's umbrella of providers.Membership Benefits

name:
Kopp Foundation for Diabetes at USCRecognized Student Organization (RSO)
							 
								- Health & Wellness, Social Awareness, ServiceWebsiteMissionMembership BenefitsMissionOur mission is to empower students to contribute meaningfully to the T1D community, upholding the foundation’s theme of  “by youth, for youth.” We aim to create opportunities for community service, mentorship, outreach, and research while providing a supportive space for students passionate about healthcare and diabetes awareness. Through collaboration, education, and action, our chapter strives to make a lasting impact both on campus and beyond—driving progress in diabetes care and shaping the next generation of healthcare leaders inspired by the fight against Type 1 Diabetes.Membership BenefitsMembers will have access to internships, scholarship and networking opportunities provided by the Kopp Foundation for Diabetes non-profit organization. Connect with USC faculty and healthcare professionals from KFD National through panels, workshops, and collaborative events. On campus, general meetings will be held to teach members about Type 1 Diabetes and other health-related issues. Members will have the opportunity to participate in meaningful service and leadership experiences that strengthen applications for health, policy, and research careers hosted by KFD at USC. Members will receive guidance on how to get into research spaces on campus and other community service pathways from established researchers from USC and KFD National. name:
League of United Latin American Citizens at the University of Southern CaliforniaRecognized Student Organization (RSO)
							 
								- Ethnic/Cultural, Civic Engagement, Political, Social Awareness, Career, ServiceWebsiteMissionMembership BenefitsMissionOur Collegiate Council of LULAC is dedicated to advancing the educational attainment, political influence, and the civil rights of Latinos through impactful community service projects. The League of United Latin American Citizens is the oldest Latino civil rights organization in the United States, and our Council seeks to carry on their legacy by mobilizing and serving our local community. Our primary goal is to address educational disparities and provide resources to public schools and nonprofits that allow students to strive academically. In addition, we aim to promote civic engagement by spreading awareness about issues facing Latinos, increasing voter registration, and hearing paths to success from empowering Latino speakers. We aim to provide USC students with access to career advancement opportunities, provided by LULAC National such as invitations to National LULAC conferences and the ability to apply for internships and scholarships.Membership BenefitsLULAC at USC will provide career advancement opportunities. (1) National Conferences: Members will be given invitations to Emerge DC, the Annual LULAC National Conventions, and local conferences and events. (2) Scholarship Opportunities: LULAC offers scholarships for higher education and stipends to attend conferences. (3) Networking: Members will listen to and connect with successful Latino leaders, politicians, and policy makers. In addition, members will have access to career development sessions, and opportunities for LULAC National internships.

name:
Ludus RemediumRecognized Student Organization (RSO)
							 
								- Social Awareness, Visual & Performing ArtsWebsiteMissionMembership BenefitsContact:Helen CulpepperMissionLudus Remedium is USC’s first freeform, activism-focused comedy troupe that includes everything from improv to sketch to sock puppets to supporting student mental health campus-wide! Wait, what was that last one? For those of you who took Latin in High School and nabbed those extra points on the SAT, you might notice that Ludus means “play” or “prank” and remedium roughly translates to “cure” or “remedy.” Our mission statement is: To cure the USC community of anxiety, intolerance, injustice (and maybe indigestion) through comedy. Completing this statement could be as simple as making a stressed out student laugh at one of our weekly shows or as complex as increasing visibility on a marginalized issue through our monthly comedy through social justice activities (we make no promises on the matter of indigestion). We aim to build confidence and bust bigotry through healthy, humorous releases. Ludus has built an impressive array of specific games, events, and traditions to make that happen.Membership Benefits1. Regular performance and writing opportunities2. A community of fellow comedic artists3. A greater platform to promote social justice issues personal to any given member4. The chance to make a difference in the wider USC community using the performing arts
mission: Ludus Remedium is USC’s first freeform, activism-focused comedy troupe that includes everything from improv to sketch to sock puppets to supporting student mental health campus-wide! name: McCarthy Honors Residential CollegeResidential EducationWebsiteMissionContact:Thomas WlodekMissionMcCarthy Honors Residential College is a vibrant, tight-knit community of scholars who share a commitment to rigorous intellectual and academic achievement. McCarthy Honors Residential College provides a bridge between the residential and academic experience. Students will engage in programs and conversations that promote innovative thinking and challenge them to explore all branches of knowledge. Students living in McCarthy Honors Residential College will have direct contact with Faculty in Residence as well as faculty, advisors, and experts across disciplines. Unique experiences in this Residential College will include connecting students to research opportunities and an early move-in summit focusing on community building and the holistic development of high achieving students. name:
Mental Health Awareness for Education StaffRecognized Student Organization (RSO)
							 
								- Health & Wellness, ServiceWebsiteMissionMembership BenefitsMissionMHAES is a student-led, experience focused program designed to provide education staff with a better understanding of various mental health issues from the perspective of previous students. Each curriculum is designed by students with personal experience with that specific mental health topic, and all material is reviewed and approved by a relevant psychology professional. Student support staff are often some of the first individuals to be notified when a student is struggling with some sort of emotional/psychological issue. While they are not directly responsible for providing mental health care, they are at the front lines of protecting students’ mental health. Our mission is to provide accurate education on the unique symptoms, struggles, and treatments that various psychological conditions receive in order to allow staff to facilitate conversations with students in a productive and empathetic manner, as well as to direct students to appropriate resources and/or professionals. Furthermore, many mental health conditions are surrounded by stigma, stereotypes, and shame in today’s society. This has led to a widespread fundamental misunderstanding of many mental health conditions, which inhibit many individuals’ ability to accurately recognize signs, facilitate conversations, and refer appropriate help to students suffering with these conditions. By having students with personal experience design the curriculum, we tear down these stereotypes and allow insight into what these conditions can really look like for students and the impacts they can have. Through this method, we hope to provide accurate and accessible education on what these conditions can look like and what education staff can do to help.Membership BenefitsThis program provides members with the opportunity to make a difference in the community by providing free, student-led and mental health professional-approved education on various mental health topics. Students will develop strong presentation skills and enhanced knowledge of mental health topics, and may even design their own presentation on a topic that is close to them.
mission: MHAES is a student-led, experience focused program designed to provide education staff with a better understanding of various mental health issues from the perspective of previous students. Our mission is to foster curiosity and confidence by bringing interactive science activities to non-traditional education settings such as community centers, especially in underserved areas. By combining education with creativity, Mini Minds empowers children to see science as something exciting, approachable, and part of everyday life.Membership BenefitsMini Minds is a student-led organization at USC dedicated to making science accessible, engaging, and inspiring for young children through hands-on, play-based learning. Our mission is to foster curiosity and confidence by bringing interactive science activities to non-traditional education settings such as community centers, particularly in underserved areas. We aim to host monthly outreach sessions serving 30–40 children per semester, designing activities that connect everyday experiences with scientific concepts. We achieve these goals by collaborating with local community centers, developing culturally inclusive lesson plans, and training USC volunteers to teach through creativity and play. Our mission guides every aspect of Mini Minds, ensuring that each activity reflects our commitment to making science an approachable and exciting part of children’s lives.

name:
Model United Nations of Southern CaliforniaRecognized Student Organization (RSO)
							 
								- Pre-Professional, Academic, PoliticalWebsiteMissionMembership BenefitsContact:Sabeeh Mirza,Email group officersMissionIt all started when a group of like-minded students felt the need for a community where the world's most pressing issues could be discussed, debated, and (theoretically) resolved. Model United Nations of Southern California was formed in 2008 to meet this need. Since then, MUNSC has grown to be an organization involving nearly 100 students from a wide variety of backgrounds and one that is ranked #15 in the nation. Our team travels all around North America to compete in conferences with other universities, as well as hosts two of our own conferences: the prestigious SCMUN for middle and high school students, and our intercollegiate TrojanMUN, ranked the top Fall conference on the West Coast by Best Delegate. Model UN not only gives newfound knowledge on global affairs, but it improves critical thinking, writing, communication, public speaking, and diplomacy skills. Aside from traveling to conferences, MUNSC members are instantly welcomed into the family with events like bonding retreats, mixers, and social events. MUNSC is filled with some of the most innovative, intelligent, and passionate students you'll ever meet - it's a choice you will never regret.Membership BenefitsAs a member of the Model UN team, you’ll gain valuable skills in public speaking, negotiation, and diplomacy. You’ll have the opportunity to engage in debates on global issues, improving your research and critical thinking abilities. Participation in conferences allows you to collaborate with peers from different schools and backgrounds, expanding your network. You’ll also develop leadership and teamwork skills, which are beneficial in both academic and professional settings.

name: New North Residential CollegeResidential EducationWebsiteMissionContact:Jake LafleurMissionNew North Residential College, originally called North Residential College, was founded in 1993 and consisted of EVK, College, University, and Harris Halls. In 1997, New Residential College (consisting of just EVK and College Halls) was split off, and for the following 18 years, New and North were separate, each with their own Faculty Masters and programming. Past Faculty Masters and residents included Professor Florence Clark, Stanley Rosen, Stephen Toulmin, Cynthia Hendricks and Tim Gustafson. Past Faculty Masters of New North Residential College are Professors Phil Ethington and Ange-Marie Hancock. Professor Ethington began as Faculty Master of New in 2011, and Professor Hancock began as Faculty master of North in 2012. Considering that the student residents of New and North shared a common identity and common spaces, Ethington and Hancock decided to re-merge the four Halls once again but retain the historic dual name, which students had long ago adopted: “New North.” New North Residential College houses predominantly first-year residents and strives to integrate a dynamic and active social setting with high academic achievement. New North Residential College is in a premium location on campus- you can grab coffee at the Starbucks at Trojan Grounds on your three-minute walk to Leavey Library, toss a Frisbee on McCarthy Quad, and appreciate the view of the neighboring reflecting pool. Across the street is the Galen Center and the historic Shrine Auditorium. New North also benefits from having the EVK dining hall attached to their building, so residents connect with a diverse group of students from all over campus.Membership Benefits

name:
NomadSCRecognized Student Organization (RSO)
							 
								- Ethnic/Cultural, Pre-Professional, Political, Social Awareness, Career, Service, SocialWebsiteMissionMembership BenefitsMissionNomadUSC exists to give Central Asian students at USC the platform we currently lack. Although many of us are here, we are not represented by existing cultural groups-neither by the Slavic Club, for we are not Slavic, but Turkic and Iranic (an extremely important distinction), nor by APASA, for we are Eurasian. Our cultures, histories, and experiences sit in their own unique space, and NomadUSC was created to finally give that identity a home on campus.Our mission begins with community. We bring Central Asian students together to create a warm, familiar environment that feels like home despite being thousands of miles away. Through our events, traditions, and shared culture, we give students the comfort, belonging, and support that every international and diaspora student deserves.From that community, we grow. We seek to foster academic and professional development by inviting alumni, diplomats, public servants, human rights advocates, and leaders from across the post-Soviet world. We create space for meaningful political, social, and economic discussions-centered on the concrete steps our generation must take as future professionals to strengthen and uplift our countries. Members learn from one another, connect with mentors, and develop the confidence to think critically about Central Asia’s future.And eventually, the circle completes itself. As we grow into educated, globally minded professionals, we will represent USC back home-in our industries, our governments, our cities, and our cultural communities. NomadUSC becomes the bridge: connecting Central Asia to USC, and USC back to Central Asia, through the students who pass through this club.Membership BenefitsMembers of NomadUSC gain access to a strong, active Central Asian community at USC. Benefits include:A tight-knit cultural community where members can connect with others who share Central Asian backgrounds, languages, and traditions. Belonging.Regular social events such as tailgates, potlucks, picnics, stargazing nights, UFC fight-night watch parties, and frequent Mafia game sessions.Cultural programming that celebrates Kazakh and broader Central Asian identity through food, music, language, and shared traditions.Academic and career support, including mentorship from upper-class students, course recommendations, internship advice, and connections to Central Asian professionals in Los Angeles, New York, and Washington.Member-only group chats and updates, providing priority access to events, activities, and volunteering opportunities.Leadership opportunities to help plan events, shape the club’s direction, and contribute to building a long-term USC cultural organization.A home away from home, offering community, support, and a sense of belonging for international students and anyone interested in Central Asia.
categories: and connections to Central Asian professionals in Los Angeles

Parkinson's PalsRecognized Student Organization (RSO)
							 
								- Civic Engagement, Health & Wellness, Service, SocialWebsiteMissionMembership BenefitsMissionParkinson’s Pals aims to reduce loneliness and social isolation among people living with Parkinson’s disease by fostering intergenerational friendships between USC students and community members affected by the condition. The organization seeks to bridge neuroscience, compassion, and service by creating meaningful relationships that enhance emotional well-being, challenge stigma, and promote awareness of Parkinson’s disease within and beyond the USC community.Membership BenefitsMembers of Parkinson’s Pals gain hands-on experience in community engagement, neurodegenerative disease awareness, and patient interaction. Through structured training, mentorship, and direct pairing with individuals living with Parkinson’s disease, students develop empathy, active-listening skills, and intergenerational communication experience that directly complement careers in healthcare, psychology, and public health. Members also benefit from leadership opportunities, partnerships with the Davis Phinney Foundation, and the chance to contribute to evidence-based service work that measurably reduces isolation and improves patient well-being.

name: Parkside Arts & Humanities Residential CollegeResidential EducationWebsiteMissionContact:Marie MontoyaMissionOn August 15, 2007, after nearly two years of construction at the cost $58 million, USC celebrated the opening of the Parkside Arts and Humanities Residential College (PRB) with the marching band, balloons and banners. “Student residences aren’t just places to sleep anymore,” said Michael L. Jackson, Vice Provost of Student Affairs, at the ceremony. “They’re part of the educational program.” Precisely for these reasons, PRB was designed to create a space where students and faculty can form a strong scholarly community centered on the arts and humanities. Although intended for students of all disciplines, its theme allows for special interest floors dedicated to music, the visual arts, poetry and architecture. Living in Parkside Arts & Humanities Residential College is not limited to majors in the arts and humanities. Everyone who appreciates the role of culture and the exploration of ideas is welcome.Standing four stories tall and bordered by Exposition Boulevard, Watt Way and 37th Place, PRB is at the southwestern end of the University Park Campus, across from the Los Angeles Natural History Museum. Joining its two sister structures, Parkside International Residential College (IRC) and Parkside Apartments, to complete the Parkside Community. Residents live in suite-style apartments, amidst state-of-the-art study lounges, music study rooms, recreation room with ping-pong, foosball and air hockey tables, and a Performance Café, all designed in response to student requests for more shared space. Outside, PRB boasts its own green space, quad, community garden and outdoor gym workout station. PRB is also a few steps away from the Parkside Restaurant, known across campus for the high quality of its food.Membership Benefits
mission: On August 15, 2007, after nearly two years of construction at the cost $58 million, USC celebrated the opening of the Parkside Arts and Humanities Residential College (PRB) with the marching band, balloons and banners. “Student residences aren’t just places to sleep anymore,” said Michael L. Jackson

name: Parkside International Residential CollegeResidential EducationWebsiteMissionContact:Estrella CruzMissionParkside International Residential College (IRC) opened in 2001, the brainchild of USC President Steven B. Sample and V.P. of Student Affairs Michael Jackson. At 295,000 square feet and four floors, it was given its theme in order to create dialogue on global issues, and to bring together international and domestic residents in a single vibrant community. Located in the lower left quadrant of the university park campus, bordered by Vermont Avenue, Exposition Boulevard, Watt Way and 37th Place, IRC provides generous suite-style living, a high quality restaurant downstairs, laundry and rec room on site, ample study space and lounge space, music practice rooms, performance café, classroom space, and an unmatched outdoor green space including fountain, fire pit, and community garden.Special Interest Floors in IRC include the Women in Science and Engineering (WiSE) Floor. While IRC is home to predominantly first-years students, Parkside Apartments (PKS) houses primarily students in their junior and senior years. Parkside Apartments is comprised of two adjoining six-story wings of two-person apartments, all with individual kitchenettes and bathrooms, offering a more independent living environment. Renovated in 2005, it was originally built to coincide with the 1984 Olympics and was part of the athletes’ Olympic Village. PKS also has an ongoing commitment to sustainability through recycling programs and the creation of the Michael Waterman Community Garden. Living at Parkside allows residents to participate in intellectual, cultural and social exchanges with people who have a wide range of backgrounds and interests, in one of the most attractive locations on campus. The physical beauty, cultural richness, and wealth of study opportunities outside the classroom make the Parkside community at USC special. They are a unique collaborative learning center, and a place where Trojans are proud to live.Membership Benefits
mission: Parkside International Residential College (IRC) opened in 2001, the brainchild of USC President Steven B. Sample and V.P. of Student Affairs Michael Jackson. Phi Chi ThetaRecognized Student Organization (RSO)
							 
								- Pre-Professional, Academic, Career, Service, SocialWebsiteMissionMembership BenefitsMissionThe mission of Xi, Collegiate Chapter of Phi Chi Theta, is to cultivate the professional leadership skills essential for success in the business community through fostering values, skills, and abilities suited for a rapidly evolving world; serving as the crossroads for people to share resources, ideas and concepts, on a local and national level; promoting professionalism, fraternity, and service among its members; instilling in its membership those values, codes, and creeds which will enable participation in a dynamic world, and equip members with the business astuteness necessary to achieve high esteem and success in their chosen fields.Membership BenefitsNationalConferences & Events: National leadership conference, regional meetups, and professional development sessions.Professional Network: Access to a nationwide network of students and alumni across business and economics.Career Resources: Job boards, internship postings, and national webinars.Cross-Chapter Collaboration: Opportunities to work with chapters across the country (4+ in CA alone).Lifetime Membership: Continuing access to the network and alumni support after graduation.Local (USC Xi Chapter)Career Exploration: Help figuring out interests, sectors, and long-term goals in business/economics.Speaker Events & Employer Connections: Direct access to recruiters, professionals, and internship opportunities.Recruitment Preparation: Resume/LinkedIn help, interview prep, and structured recruiting timelines.Professional Development: Workshops on resume and LinkedIn development, communication, presentations, and workplace soft skills.Academic Support: Study sessions, tutoring, and course guidance.Community & Culture: Supportive peer network, big-little mentorship, and strong chapter culture.Leadership Opportunities: Roles in operations, committees, event planning, and chapter management.Social Events: Mixers, bonding activities, retreats, and chapter traditions.Philanthropy: Opportunities to give back to the community and participate in service events.Many students join Phi Chi Theta feeling uncertain about their career path and discouraged by how competitive other business clubs can feel. Our chapter takes a different approach. We focus on character, curiosity, and growth rather than air-tight resumes. Members come here to explore what they want to do, build confidence, develop real professional skills, and find a supportive community that helps them reach their potential.

name:
Public Relations Student Society of AmericaRecognized Student Organization (RSO)
							 
								- Pre-Professional, Academic, CareerWebsiteMissionMembership BenefitsMissionThe purpose of PRSSA is to cultivate a favorable and mutually advantageous relationship between students and professional public relations practitioners. PRSSA aims to serve its members by enhancing their knowledge of public relations, providing access to professional development opportunities, and broadening their professional network in preparation for their careers after graduation. PRSSA aspires to develop ethically responsible pre-professionals who champion diversity, strive for an outstanding education and advocate for the profession.Membership BenefitsStudent-Run PR FirmOur award-winning PR firm PRSC is entirely student-run and provides members with real opportunities to learn and practice critical PR skills outside the classroom. Students work on PR and marketing campaigns in real-time for clients ranging from local restaurants and student films to internationally recognized brands. Some of our past and present clients include Warner Bros Discovery, Apple TV+ and NASCAR. Applications for PRSC open at the beginning of each semester and members of any class standing and level of experience are encouraged to apply.Agency ToursThe best way for students to experience life at a PR agency is to get a peek inside. We bring members to leading agencies across Los Angeles to meet their teams and learn what it’s like to work at an agency. Our last tour was with the Ayzenberg Group, an advertising and marketing firm in Pasadena specializing in the video game market!Speaker Panels and Networking EventsNo matter what your field of interest is within the PR space, we’ve got you covered. We regularly host speaker panels where we bring in top PR pros from different industries to give members a look into their field of expertise. Moderated by PRSSA officers, attendees have the chance to ask panelists their own questions in a Q&A at the end. Past panels include Sports, Lifestyle, Entertainment and more. We also hold networking events where we invite several alumni to come and chat with members in a speed-dating-style session.Skill WorkshopsEach fall, we hold a series of PR skill workshops called “masterclasses” where professionals, interns and PRSSA upperclassmen teach members critical industry tools that aren’t often taught in the classroom. Previous topics include resumes, cover letters, interviews, email newsletters and portfolio sites.Career OpportunitiesAside from numerous networking opportunities with potential employers, members have access to the PRSA Jobcenter and Internship Center where you can explore career opportunities targeted at PRSSA members. Members also have access to the exclusive USC PRSSA LinkedIn group where we share internship and job opportunities as well as guest speakers’ contact information.MentorshipOur mentorship program allows members to connect with each other socially and build their professional networks. At the beginning of the year, we pair underclassmen with upperclassmen and grad students based on personal and professional interests. Mentorship pairs connect throughout the year with fun and engaging events and competitions, becoming friends and peers. Applications for the program open in the fall.Bonding EventsWhat better way to get to know our community than with fun social activities? We hold a wide range of events from mixers to Hollywood movie premieres where members can meet each other in a non-professional setting. We’re also very close with our fellow SoCal chapters including Cal State Fullerton and Cal Poly Pomona, and we plan at least one social event with them per semester. Last year we had a beach bonfire and went to an LA Angels game together!Scholarships and AwardsPRSSA members have exclusive access to award recognition and some scholarships through the PRSA Foundation and priority consideration for other scholarships, such as the PRSA-LA Scholarship. Scholarship and award applications generally open and close in the spring.Leadership PositionsOne of the best ways to get involved with our chapter is to join our Executive Board. Officers on the Board are responsible for keeping our chapter up and running with roles ranging from managing finances to alumni outreach. Positions include President, VP of Internal Affairs, VP of External Affairs, Director of PR, Director of Outreach, Director of Professional Development, Treasurer, Secretary and Social Media Manager. The recruitment process for each school year takes place at the end of the previous year, with officers finalized in April. Executive Board members have the opportunity to attend the PRSSA International Conference (ICON) and the National Leadership Assembly.Post-Grad SupportOur parent organization, PRSA, makes it easy for students to transition from our chapter to theirs upon entering the workforce.
categories: One of the best ways to get involved with our chapter is to join our Executive Board. Officers on the Board are responsible for keeping our chapter up and running with roles ranging from managing finances to alumni outreach. Positions include President

mission: The purpose of PRSSA is to cultivate a favorable and mutually advantageous relationship between students and professional public relations practitioners. We aim to create a professional and social community for influencers, digital marketers, photographers, and anyone interested in social media.Membership BenefitsBuild A FollowingLearn how to develop your image online and grow your audience. Whether you're a creator with 10M followers or a creator with 10K followers, there's always something to learn at Reach.Create Viral ContentCollaboration is key in the social media world and joining the Reach network will give you an advantage in the content creation game. With fun Reach socials and creator days,Growth Hack a BrandLearn how to grow a brand, your brand. Whether you're on the industry side of things or the influencer side of things, it is important to know how to market your brand and find the right audience.Work With Major BrandsWhen we say Reach is a family, we truly mean it. Reach members are actively matching other members with world-class brands. Boost your revenue and exposure as you do what you love.Hear Guest SpeakersFrom top creators to industry professionals, every meeting has something to teach you. We've had speakers from Google, Fullscreen, WarnerMedia, and more. Learn from industry experts and start networking with those who know the game.
mission: Reach is the first and only social media club at USC. But more than an org, we're a family! We aim to create a professional and social community for influencers, digital marketers, photographers, and anyone interested in social media.

Our lead-based mentorship structure connects new members with experienced teammates, providing guidance in both technical development and career navigation. By working alongside passionate, technically skilled peers to build real-world systems, our members graduate with the kind of experience that stands out in a competitive job market.

name:
SC TriathlonRecreational Club Council (RCC)
							 
								- Health & Wellness, Recreation, Club Sport, SocialWebsiteMissionMembership BenefitsContact:Email group officersMissionHello & welcome to the SC Triathlon family! Our mission at SC Triathlon is to help USC students learn about, enjoy, and compete in the sport of triathlon through training and intercollegiate competition. The SC Triathlon team hosts weekly practices for beginners all the way to advanced triathletes. Our team is led by Coach Corey Norris, a USA Triathlon certified coach with a successful history in the sport of triathlon.Membership BenefitsWhat’s included with a tri membership? Access to …A coach (Some fun facts about Corey: He was SCTri president as an undergrad and has completed 4 Ironman’s!)Team gear including bikes, wetsuits, car bike racks, etc.Discounted race registration on courses across California (including Santa Barbara, San Luis Obispo, and Malibu)Transportation and housing at races (This makes paying dues worth it after just one race!)Daily workouts that are never required but provide the opportunity to train to complete a sprint/olympic distance triathlonDiscounted gear, bike maintenance, nutrition, etc. from our sponsors including Skratch, Roka, Rudy Project, and more! Our sponsorship packet will be released soon for due paying membersOpportunities to workout all around Los AngelesAn awesome group of people who are extremely dedicated to working out (each with a different level of experience and topic of expertise!)
mission: Hello & welcome to the SC Triathlon family! The SC Triathlon team hosts weekly practices for beginners all the way to advanced triathletes.

name:
Sigma Phi DeltaRecognized Student Organization (RSO)
							 
								- Pre-Professional, Academic, SocialWebsiteMissionMembership BenefitsContact:Benjamin Lindstrom,Email group officersMissionSigma Phi Delta serves as a premier social-professional fraternity for students pursuing careers in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM). As a values-based organization, we aim to support members in achieving academic excellence, honing professional competencies, and forming meaningful connections.Our membership fosters an environment where members can flourish personally and professionally. We provide opportunities for members to network with fellow STEM students, professors, and industry professionals. This allows members to build strong professional relationships and gain mentorship opportunities.At the core of our organization is a spirit of service, collaboration, and lifelong brotherhood. Our organization unites members around shared goals and values. We promote integrity, responsible citizenship, and commitment to advancing the STEM fields.Members are encouraged to participate in professional development workshops, networking events, community service initiatives, and social activities. These experiences allow members to strengthen communication, teamwork, and leadership abilities.By joining Sigma Phi Delta, members gain a supportive community that motivates them to reach their full potential. Our alumni network contains over 13,000 accomplished professionals who provide mentorship and career assistance. We empower members to work hard in the classroom, develop professionally, play hard with friends, and create unforgettable memories that will last beyond their university years.Membership BenefitsAt Sigma Phi Delta’s Alpha Chapter at USC, we offer our members more than just a fraternity. We offer them a chance to grow as engineers and as individuals, by providing them with various opportunities and benefits, such as:Exclusive social and brotherhood events, such as hikes, retreats, beach days, and barbecues, where they can bond with their fellow brothers and have fun. Our fraternity is built on the values of friendship, loyalty, and service, and we foster a strong sense of community and belonging among our members.Networking events, professional development, and resume workshops, where they can learn from and connect with our vast alumni network of engineers that spans from 1924 till the present day. Our alumni have worked in diverse fields and industries, and are eager to share their insights and experiences with our members.Leadership opportunities, where they can take on roles and responsibilities within the fraternity and make a positive impact. Our members can run for executive positions, lead committees, organize events, mentor new members, and more. By being involved in the fraternity, our members develop valuable skills and qualities that will help them succeed in their future endeavors.Academic support, where they can receive help and guidance from their brothers on their coursework, projects, and research. Our members have access to our study rooms, library, tutoring sessions, and peer review groups. We also encourage our members to collaborate and innovate with each other on engineering challenges and competitions.

name: South Residential CollegeResidential EducationWebsiteMissionContact:David SanchezMissionOpened as a residential college in 2012, the South Residential College includes four traditional-style residence halls: Marks Tower, Pardee Tower, Marks Hall (closed for 2022-2023) and Trojan Hall (closed for 2022-2023). A signature of this residential area is its close-knit community; many describe South as being a true “family.”The South Residential Colleges are supported by an engaging team of faculty and staff who endeavor to create a vibrant community where emphasis is placed on the transition to college and exploring USC’s First Year Experience programs. Programming provided by faculty and staff emphasizes personal growth and determining one’s best pathway for leadership as a USC student and future graduate. Residents are encouraged to attend Faculty in Residence events and participate in faculty-student interaction outside the classroom. Students in South have always benefited from the sense of belonging that occurs within these facilities which offer the smallest floor communities at USC. Special Interest floors in South include APIDA Leaders, El Sol y La Luna, and Somerville Place.Membership Benefits

								- Pre-Professional, Academic, Career, ServiceWebsiteMissionMembership BenefitsMissionSouthern California Healthcare Consulting Group (SCHCG) stands as the University of Southern California’s first student-run healthcare-focused consulting organization. Our members are committed to advancing public health and healthcare access, utilizing our expertise to provide pro-bono consulting services to both nonprofit and for-profit entities in the healthcare field. Our diverse backgrounds in life sciences, business, and engineering enhance our ability to understand the complex healthcare landscape and deliver innovative solutions.At SCHCG, we envision a future where healthcare equity is paramount. Our services aim to empower our partners, helping them fulfill their missions effectively. Through collaborative efforts, we aspire to make a lasting impact on the communities we serve, contributing to a healthier, more equitable future.Membership BenefitsSince launching in 2023, Southern California Healthcare Consulting Group (SCHCG) has partnered with 10 clients internationally, providing real-lime healthcare consulting experience across various project scopes. Our organization teaches interested students the basics of healthcare consulting, technical and soft skills for healthcare consulting, and navigating recruiting into this competitive industry. In addition, we have hosted 6 guest speakers across established firms (including McKinsey, Bain & Company, PwC, and Deloitte regarding each firm's respective healthcare opportunities), and will continue to provide connections with industry experts going forward. With our niche experience in healthcare consulting and passion for expanding health equity, our members have been placed in top firms for internship and full-time opportunities, including Boston Consulting Group, Accenture, Trinity Life Sciences. As a result, each member receives mentorship through our alumni and guest speakers to help them achieve their professional aspirations.

name:
Student Association for Addiction ScienceRecognized Student Organization (RSO)
							 
								- Pre-Professional, Academic, Civic Engagement, CareerWebsiteMissionMembership BenefitsContact:Aaryan MidhaMissionThe Student Association for Addiction Science (SAAS) at USC is dedicated to fostering a supportive and collaborative community for undergraduate and graduate students passionate about addiction science. Our mission is to:Educate: Provide comprehensive education and resources about addiction science, including the latest research, treatment strategies, and policy developments.Connect: Facilitate networking and collaboration among students, faculty, and professionals to create a strong support network and enhance career development opportunities.Advocate: Promote and advocate for equitable policies related to addiction science within the USC community and beyond.Engage: Organize engaging events, workshops, and outreach programs to raise awareness about various forms of addiction, including substance use, technology, gambling, and more.Support: Offer mentorship and professional development opportunities through partnerships with alumni, community leaders, and industry experts.Join SAAS to access relevant training, multidisciplinary networking, advocacy initiatives, and professional development opportunities. Whether you are an undergraduate, graduate student, postdoctoral fellow, faculty member, staff, or alumnus from any discipline, we welcome you to be part of our community.For more information or to get involved, contact us at uscaddictionscience@gmail.com. Follow us on Instagram @usc.saas for news updates, job postings, and mentorship opportunities related to addiction science.Membership BenefitsJoining SAAS offers a range of exclusive benefits designed to enrich your academic and professional journey in the field of addiction science:Access to Educational Resources: Members receive access to a wealth of educational materials, including the latest research publications, webinars, and workshops that deepen understanding of addiction science.Networking Opportunities: Connect with a diverse network of students, faculty, alumni, and industry professionals, facilitating collaborations and mentorships that can enhance your career prospects.Professional Development: Participate in skill-building workshops, career panels, and mentorship programs tailored to prepare you for a successful career in addiction science or related fields.Advocacy and Leadership Roles: Take on leadership positions within SAAS, giving you the opportunity to spearhead advocacy initiatives and influence addiction-related policies at USC and beyond.Exclusive Events: Attend members-only events, including guest speaker sessions, roundtable discussions, and social gatherings that provide both educational value and a sense of community.Career Support: Benefit from job postings, internship opportunities, and guidance from professionals in the field, helping you navigate your career path in addiction science.Community Engagement: Engage in community outreach programs that raise awareness about addiction and contribute to meaningful change both on campus and in the broader community.

name:
Swim Club at USCRecreational Club Council (RCC)
							 
								- Club SportWebsiteMissionMembership BenefitsContact:Email group officersMissionThe Swim Club at USC is a student run recreational organization that is dedicated to growing and fostering a community for students interested in the sport of competitive swimming.Membership BenefitsJoining the **USC Swim Club** offers a wide range of benefits that cater to swimmers of all skill levels. Here are some in-depth advantages of becoming a member:1. Professional Coaching- Experienced Coaches: The club provides access to qualified and experienced coaches who offer personalized training and guidance to help you improve your technique, speed, and overall performance.- Structured Training Programs: Coaches develop structured and progressive training plans tailored to various skill levels, ensuring consistent improvement and goal achievement.- Technique Improvement: Regular feedback and technique drills help in refining strokes, starts, turns, and finishes, leading to more efficient and effective swimming.2. Access to Excellent Facilities- State-of-the-Art Pools: Members have access to USC's top-notch aquatic facilities, including Olympic-sized pools equipped for both training and competition.- Training Equipment- Availability of various swimming aids and equipment such as kickboards, pull buoys, and fins to enhance different aspects of your training.- Flexible Practice Times: Multiple practice sessions scheduled throughout the week accommodate different class schedules and personal commitments.3. Competitive Opportunities- Local and National Meets: The club participates in various competitions, providing opportunities to compete against other universities and clubs, enhancing your competitive experience.- Team Representation: Represent USC at different levels of competition, fostering school pride and personal accomplishment.- Performance Tracking: Regular meets allow you to track your progress, set new personal records, and receive recognition for your achievements.4. Health and Fitness Benefits- Full-Body Workout: Swimming engages all major muscle groups, improving cardiovascular health, strength, flexibility, and endurance.- Low-Impact Exercise: Ideal for reducing stress on joints while still providing an effective workout, suitable for injury prevention and rehabilitation.- Mental Well-being: Regular swimming is known to reduce stress, improve mood, and boost mental clarity, contributing to overall well-being.5. Social and Community Engagement- **Like-Minded Peers**: Connect with fellow students who share a passion for swimming, fostering friendships and a supportive community.- Team-Building Activities: Participate in social events, group outings, and team-building exercises that enhance camaraderie and create lasting college memories.- Networking Opportunities: Build connections that can extend beyond college, benefiting future personal and professional endeavors.6. Leadership and Personal Development- Club Leadership Roles: Opportunities to take on positions such as Team Captain, Event Coordinator, or Treasurer, developing valuable leadership and organizational skills.- Event Planning Experience: Gain experience in organizing meets, social events, and fundraising activities, enhancing project management and teamwork abilities.- Volunteer and Community Service: Engage in community outreach programs and volunteer opportunities facilitated by the club, contributing positively to society and building your resume.7. Flexible Commitment Levels- Adaptable Participation: Whether you're a competitive swimmer or prefer recreational swimming, the club accommodates various commitment levels and goals.- Inclusive Environment: Welcoming atmosphere for swimmers of all backgrounds and skill levels, encouraging participation and enjoyment of the sport.- Balance with Academics: The club understands academic priorities and offers flexibility to ensure you can balance studies and extracurricular involvement effectively.8. Enhancement of College Experience- Skill Enhancement: Continuously develop and hone a lifelong skill that promotes a healthy lifestyle.- Resume Building: Active participation and leadership roles in the club can significantly enhance your resume, demonstrating commitment, teamwork, and leadership qualities to future employers.
categories: - Balance with Academics: The club understands academic priorities and offers flexibility to ensure you can balance studies and extracurricular involvement effectively.

name:

								- Pre-Professional, CareerWebsiteMissionMembership BenefitsContact:Email group officersMissiontechie is an undergraduate USC Recognized Student Organization for Trojans pursuing business roles within the tech industry.Membership Benefitstechie is an undergraduate USC Recognized Student Organization for Trojans pursuing business roles within the tech industry. techie aims to aid students in their journey into tech through extensive resources, opportunities, and networks. Every semester, we organize guest speaker panel events with industry professionals working in roles such as business strategy, marketing, finance, product management, talent acquisition/recruiting, etc.—from companies like Google, Amazon, Meta/Facebook, TikTok, LinkedIn, Twitter, Xbox, and more! In the past, techie has also led a mentorship program, held professional development workshops, provided exclusive professional network connections and job application opportunities, and hosted social events (including an annual techie retreat). This school year, we will continue to offer our members our various exploratory opportunities, as well as client services projects with companies within the tech industry in which student analysts can exercise their learnings in a semester-long, real-world experience. To optimize membership value and professional network utility, events are only open to techie members.
mission: techie is an undergraduate USC Recognized Student Organization for Trojans pursuing business roles within the tech industry.

								- Ethnic/Cultural, Pre-Professional, Academic, Health & Wellness, CareerWebsiteMissionMembership BenefitsMissionAt The Collective Identity Mentoring, we believe in the power of Black women and non-gender conforming individuals, and our mission is to go beyond helping them survive—to uplift and support their will to thrive. We are dedicated to creating a psychosocial safe space for Black women and non-gender conforming individuals to explore who they are and how they identify, through one-on-one mentoring and programs that help them define their path in life.Our purpose is to teach USC Black women and non-gender conforming students the value of networking, mentorship, and guidance, all within a supportive and nurturing environment. We believe that through connection and growth, we can provide them with the space they need to fully discover and embrace who they are as individuals.Membership BenefitsThe Collective Identity hosts workshops and events that promote community among Black women and Black gender expansive individuals who are current USC students. Our workshops include guest speaker presentations, Q&As, and bonding activities. All workshops will touch upon the basics skills of time management, communication, boundaries, nutrition and more. TCI provides open membership for all USC students and USC staff, faculty, alumni, and alumnae and general membership for those who complete the following: submit an application, participate in a screening interview, and submit any applicable membership dues. General members are referred to as mentees who receive access to mentorship, excursions, and mentee-only workshops and events that promote individual and professional growth within their personal and professional relationships.

name:
The Medica Zone at USCRecognized Student Organization (RSO)
							 
								- Pre-Professional, Academic, Health & Wellness, ServiceWebsiteMissionMembership BenefitsMissionThe Medica Zone at USC serves as a foundation for preparing and empowering future healthcare professionals and leaders from diverse backgrounds, grounded in the unique strengths of USC’s academic community and the vibrant, multicultural landscape of Los Angeles. By combining health education, community engagement, and accessible public-health communication, the organization equips students with the knowledge and cultural awareness needed to serve communities across LA and beyond. Through mentorship and a commitment to health equity, The Medica Zone at USC cultivates compassionate leaders who are ready to address disparities and expand access to trustworthy health information.Membership BenefitsMembership in The Medica Zone at USC offers students meaningful opportunities to grow as future health leaders while making a direct impact on community well-being. Members gain hands-on experience creating accessible health-education materials for real audiences, participate in service projects and outreach events across Los Angeles, and receive mentorship from professionals in medicine, public health, and nonprofit leadership. The organization provides workshops on health communication, advocacy, and career development, along with leadership positions that allow students to shape programming and guide community partnerships. Above all, members join a collaborative, equity-focused pre-health community committed to improving health literacy and expanding access to trustworthy information.
