name:
The Mustard Seed ProjectRecognized Student Organization (RSO)
							 
								- Civic Engagement, Social Awareness, Service, Environmental/SustainabilityWebsiteMissionMembership BenefitsContact:Chichi Makasi,Madison Palmer,Justin ShihMissionThe Mustard Seed Project is an innovative volunteer-based service for individuals experiencing homelessness; our mission is to provide low barrier access to basic needs and services for displaced communities around the USC campus & greater LA area. We foster a person-centered approach to homelessness services, and apply best practice and data driven techniques to homeless outreach. We hope to develop young leaders in professionalism, political competency, and ethics while educating the public, and the USC student body, about homelessness in their respective communities.The Mustard Seed Project, which is affiliated with the International Mustard Seed Project Organization, follows a mission guided by a “hand up, not hand out” philosophy, to connect unsheltered individuals to services and programs through community organization, relationship-based outreach, and volunteer case management.We hope to direct outreach and volunteer projects across Los Angeles to distribute basic supplies such as food and water to displaced populations; we will also work with our Chapter Advisors and the Directors of MSP to disseminate housing, pro-bono consulting, and work opportunities to displaced populations.Membership BenefitsUSC Mustard Seed Project defines membership on three different levels; Open Membership, General/Voting Membership, and Executive Membership.Open membership is not restricted by dues, interviews, or application process – this entails that all members of the USC student body are free to join MSP as an Open Member without being bound by any restrictions. Open Members are able to attend all General Member meetings, and learn about homelessness in the Greater Los Angeles area. They will also be able to assist in the planning of volunteer events, and partake in two volunteer events per semester. Open Members will be able to learn about the legal, health, and economic implications of displacement in their community, enabling them to educate the rest of the USC student body about the mission of The Mustard Seed Project.General/Voting Members must pay dues of $30.00USD/semester in order to be eligible to vote on matters concerning MSPSC, and have a larger stake in the planning and execution of events. On top of sharing the benefits of Open Membership, these members will also be able to attend more than two volunteer events a semester, and reap the benefits of hands-on and in-field relief with displaced communities in the GTLA area.Executive Membership is limited to the Officers of the Mustard Seed Project; these positions are outlined in the organization's Constitution, and require a formal application and interview process. The details regarding this process can be found in the MSPSC Constitution. These members must still pay the $30.00USD/semester dues required for General Membership, but have the largest stake in planning and executing events. These members are required to attend all General Member meetings, as well as Executive Board meetings, and are responsible for overseeing the wellbeing of the organization.
mission: The Mustard Seed Project is an innovative volunteer-based service for individuals experiencing homelessness; our mission is to provide low barrier access to basic needs and services for displaced communities around the USC campus & greater LA area. We foster a person-centered approach to homelessnes

name:
The NucleusRecognized Student Organization (RSO)
							 
								- Academic, Design Team, Health & Wellness, Media/Publications, Social Awareness, Environmental/SustainabilityWebsiteMissionMembership BenefitsContact:Yj Si,Maaha Hashmani,Email group officersMissionThe mission of The Nucleus is to present the latest in science and technology in an easily digestible manner and to maintain accessibility for all readers. Our students will be the voice of scientific information literacy within the USC student body and prepare and publish media that represents a community driven by a desire to have an impact on global well-being. We encourage our students to find inspiration in everything, and to put together media that ties these elements back to a scientific rational foundation. We will remain dedicated to serving the USC student body by means of credibility, transparency, and community.Membership BenefitsMembers of The Nucleus will be part of a community of students passionate about improving information literacy within the USC student body. Students will do so as writers, editors, and/or graphic designers publishing articles, infographics, and/or podcasts every 6 weeks. Students who join as writers and editors will have the opportunity to first author their own work in the form of short-form articles. Students who join as graphic designers will be afforded the opportunity to build their portfolio through designs for articles and infographics. Students will also be given the opportunity to be the voice of The Nucleus, through our podcast multimedia team. Students who join weekly meetings will also be provided professional development opportunities, including workshops, volunteering, and discussions with USC faculty and other guest speakers who are experts in the various fields of science, technology, and advocacy.

								- Pre-Professional, AcademicWebsiteMissionMembership BenefitsMissionThe club’s mission is to provide an educational curriculum on all things fixed income and credit, assist in recruiting preparation (primarily geared towards LevFin, credit research, and fixed income asset management), and connect students with industry professionals (we already have commitments from LevFin Analysts at JPM, Citi, and Scotia).Membership BenefitsEducational Curriculum Access: Members gain structured exposure to the fundamentals and advanced concepts of fixed income, leveraged finance, and credit research through workshops, case studies, and modeling sessions.Technical Skill Development: Training in bond valuation, credit modeling, and capital structure analysis, including Excel and market data tools (Bloomberg, CapIQ, etc.).Recruiting Preparation: Focused prep for interviews in leveraged finance, credit research, and asset management through mock interviews, resume reviews, and networking with alumni.Exclusive Networking Opportunities: Direct access to professionals and analysts at leading firms such as JPMorgan, Morgan Stanley, Citi, and Scotiabank for mentorship, speaker panels, and coffee chats.Career Mentorship: Guidance from upperclassmen and alumni who have successfully recruited into investment banking, credit research, and asset management.Community & Collaboration: Join a cohort of motivated students with shared interests in markets and credit, fostering collaboration on research projects and investment theses.

name:
Trojan Health Interpretation ServicesRecognized Student Organization (RSO)
							 
								- Academic, Civic Engagement, Health & Wellness, Social Awareness, ServiceWebsiteMissionMembership BenefitsContact:Lulu Saldana,Email group officersMissionTrojan Health Interpretation Services' primary mission is to fulfill a demand for interpreters for the Limited English Proficient population in order to guarantee medical services for all. We will provide free health interpretation and translation services for people in and around the USC area. Another primary mission is to train bilingual students to become ad hoc interpreters through extensive medical vocabulary training and practice situations. We seek to provide our services to grassroots organizations that are working with limited resources; usually, these organizations provide free services and do not have the funds or the organizational structure to have a full-time interpreter. Ultimately, Trojan Health Interpretation Services actively works to provide free ad hoc interpretation services to the vast Limited English Proficient population in Los Angeles.Membership BenefitsThere are many membership benefits from joining Trojan Health Interpretation Services (THIS). First off, THIS members will learn important medical skills and terminology in the target language which they will then be able to utilize in the future. Not only that, but they will become equipped with the tools and skills to interpret because of their interpreter etiquette training. If a member decides to pursue a career path in medicine, these two skills combined could help them become a better health professional and allow them to aid as many people as possible. Furthermore, members will be able to get an inside look at what happens in interactions between patients and physicians while simultaneously positively impacting the local Limited English Proficient community.

name:
USC School of Cinematic ArtsSchool or Department
							 
								- Academic, Campus Department, CareerWebsiteMissionMembership BenefitsContact:Benjamin PolaMissionThe mission of the USC School of Cinematic Arts is to develop and articulate the creative, scholarly and entrepreneurial principles and practices of film, television and interactive media, and in doing so to inspire and prepare the future leaders of the field.Membership BenefitsThe USC School of Cinematic Arts comprises seven divisions: Film & Television Production, Writing for Screen & Television, Animation + Digital Arts, Cinema Media Studies, Game Design, Media Arts + Practice and Producing.SCA is proud of its nearly 17,000 alumni who include some of the most notable, creative and influential individuals in the entertainment and media industries such as George Lucas, Robert Zemeckis, Ron Howard, Kevin Feige, Shonda Rhimes, Judd Apatow, the late Laura Ziskin, Ryan Coogler, Stacey Sher, Melissa Rosenberg, Prentice Penny, Jon Landau, Jay Roach, the late John Singleton, Suzanne Todd, Jennifer Todd, Lee Unkrich, Jenova Chen, Sasha Alexander, Tracy Oliver, Matt Reeves, Tim Story, and Susan Downey, among many others.
mission: The mission of the USC School of Cinematic Arts is to develop and articulate the creative, scholarly and entrepreneurial principles and practices of film, television and interactive media, and in doing so to inspire and prepare the future leaders of the field.

name:
USC Spatial Sciences InstituteSchool or Department
							 
								- AcademicWebsiteMissionMissionWe see the world differently: we view it through a spatial lens and use “where” to help address global challenges. As the hub of spatial thinking and analysis at USC, we partner with researchers, businesses, non-profits, NGOs, and other entities to analyze, model, and visualize location-based data. Our internationally-recognized faculty contribute to the rapidly-evolving body of geospatial knowledge. Through our innovative academic programs, we develop the next generation of spatial thinkers, empowering them with capabilities to solve real-world issues.Membership Benefits
mission: We see the world differently: we view it through a spatial lens and use “where” to help address global challenges. As the hub of spatial thinking and analysis at USC, we partner with researchers, businesses, non-profits, NGOs, and other entities to analyze, model, and visualize location-based data.

Volunteer Income Tax Assistance Program at the University of Southern CaliforniaRecognized Student Organization (RSO)
							 
								- Pre-Professional, ServiceWebsiteMissionMembership BenefitsContact:Email group officersMissionVolunteer Income Tax Assistance (VITA) at USC is a volunteer organization sponsored by the IRS, KYCC, Deloitte, and KPMG. VITA is dedicated to serving the Central Los Angeles community through providing free income tax return assistance to residents of the local community. Through training, and by gaining IRS certification, volunteers gain and utilize knowledge of the tax returns and various tax treaties to file income tax returns for community residents. VITA services resident and international students, allowing for a larger range of returns and specialization.Membership BenefitsJoining the Volunteer Income Tax Assistance (VITA) program at USC offers a range of valuable benefits. As a VITA volunteer, you'll gain hands-on experience in tax preparation, enhancing your understanding of the U.S. tax system, which is particularly beneficial if you're pursuing a career in accounting, finance, or law. You'll also have the opportunity to develop critical skills such as client communication, problem-solving, and attention to detail, all of which are highly valued by employers. Additionally, VITA allows you to make a meaningful impact on the community by providing free tax assistance to low-income individuals, students, and other underserved groups. This experience not only enriches your resume but also offers a sense of fulfillment from helping others. Furthermore, being involved in VITA connects you with a network of like-minded peers and professionals, opening doors to mentorship, networking opportunities, and potential internships or job placements.
mission: Volunteer Income Tax Assistance (VITA) at USC is a volunteer organization sponsored by the IRS, KYCC, Deloitte, and KPMG. name:
WorldMed @ UPCRecognized Student Organization (RSO)
							 
								- Pre-Professional, Academic, Career, Service, SocialWebsiteMissionContact:Email group officersMissionIn 2008, the WorldMed at USC: A Global Health Initiative (WorldMed) wasfounded by graduates of the inaugural class of the Master of Science in Global MedicineProgram. In 2016, WorldMed opened a new chapter at the undergraduate campus(WorldMed @ UPC). The mission of WorldMed @ UPC is to bring together a robust anddiverse community of undergraduate, graduate, and medical students, physicians, andfaculty members of the Keck School of Medicine of USC who are eager to understand,discuss, and tackle the critical issues in global medicine. As a response to the pressingneed for a comprehensive effort to address global disease, WorldMed @ UPC strives toacquire and disseminate scientific principles related to international medicine and enableits members to take initiatives in combating the global burden of disease in the localcommunity. The organization will also help other nonmembers interested in helping thiscause by providing education and resources on how to contribute personally.Membership Benefits
mission: In 2008, the WorldMed at USC: A Global Health Initiative (WorldMed) was founded by graduates of the inaugural class of the Master of Science in Global Medicine Program.
